entries.title: Commentary on Lamentations on lamentations 3:59
entries.excerpt: The benevolence of the divine judge is here accounted for. Besides, an accusation regarding the adversary is proposed. First is an accusation against the evil within the deed. "Thou hast seen the wrong done to me, O Lord; judge thou my cause." This states as if: they are unable to deny what is known to thee, (O Lord God). For, Lamentations 1:22 makes known: "Let all their evil doing come before thee; and deal with them as thou hast dealt with me."
